title: A good little EV you won't be able to buy soon: The Volvo EX30 Cross Country
gist: Volvo is halting US imports of its EX30 EV after Biden- and Trump-era tariffs on Chinese-made cars, plus a 25% EU tariff, pushed the once-$34,950 EV to near $50,000 for the Cross Country trim, illustrating how tariffs price affordable EVs out of the US market.

themes.theme: Tariffs pricing an affordable EV out of the US

insights.claim: The Volvo EX30's low price depended on cheap manufacturing in Zhangjiakou, China, but Biden-era (2024) and Trump-era tariffs on Chinese-made cars forced Volvo to shift US-destined production to Ghent, Belgium.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Tariffs are directly reshaping EV supply chains and killing low-cost import models; a structural headwind for China-built EVs in the US.
insights.locator: Its diminutive purchase price was predicated

insights.claim: A 25 percent tariff on European-made cars raised the EX30's starting price from a planned $34,950 to $40,345 (RWD) or $46,345 (AWD), with the Cross Country trim pushing near $50,000.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Quantifies the roughly $5,000-15,000 tariff-driven price inflation that eliminates the EV's value proposition.
insights.locator: However, European-made cars are still subject to a 25 percent tariff

insights.claim: Volvo has closed the EX30 order books and is stopping US imports, leaving only about 1,200 cars in inventory (roughly 250 Cross Country, fewer than 50 RWD).
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Market exit of a sub-$50k EV narrows affordable US EV supply; a signal of tariff-driven segment contraction.
insights.locator: It's a compact and efficient electric crossover

insights.claim: The average new US vehicle grew 1 inch wider and 22 inches longer between 2013 and 2023, a trend the author argues should reverse because bigger cars need more energy and cause more collision harm.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: A structural critique of vehicle bloat frames demand risk for oversized models and opportunity for efficient compacts.
insights.quote: the average new vehicle in the US grew an inch (25 mm) wider and 22 inches (558 mm) longer between 2013 and 2023
insights.locator: Did you know the average new vehicle

insights.claim: The EX30 Cross Country offers 422 hp, 0-62 mph in 3.7 seconds, a 65 kWh net battery, 153 kW DC fast charging, and just 227 miles of range.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Strong performance but modest range at a ~$50k price shows the value erosion once tariffs are added.

insights.claim: Despite announcing NACS adoption for US EVs by 2025, Volvo shipped the EX30 with a CCS1 socket, and Edmunds called its long-term ownership experience 'a very frustrating car to live with.'
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Charging-standard and reliability gaps add to the case against the model beyond just price.
insights.quote: a very frustrating car to live with
insights.locator: I should also add that long-term reports
